metadata.flavor_note: The march through the Charred Wastes to the base of the Rot Spire. This is the Blight's heartland -- the region closest to the Rot-Heart where the corruption has burned through everything, leaving only ash and the Blight's most powerful servants. The landscape is post-apocalyptic: a blasted expanse of grey ash, dead root-stumps, and the remains of settlements that were consumed decades ago.

0.name: The Charred Wastes
0.description: The Charred Wastes begin where the Burnwillow's fire-leaves stop. The canopy above is gone -- burned away, leaving raw sky visible for the first time. The sky is not blue. It is the color of old bone, streaked with grey clouds that move too deliberately to be natural. The ground is ash over dead root-bark, ankle-deep and still warm. Nothing grows here. The Blight consumed everything, then consumed itself, leaving a sterile expanse that stretches to the base of the Spire. Two Doom Heralds patrol the wastes -- tall, gaunt figures in corroded armor, their bodies sustained by the ambient Blight radiation. They were Seekers once.
0.read_aloud: The canopy is gone. For the first time in your life, you see the sky without the Burnwillow's fire-leaves between you and it. The sky is the color of bone. The ground is warm ash. Nothing grows. Nothing moves except the heat-shimmer and two shapes in the distance that move with the deliberate gait of things that have forgotten how to stop walking. They were people once. The armor is Seeker-grade. They carry weapons they no longer know how to use. They do not need weapons. They are weapons now.
0.sensory_signature: Warm ash underfoot. Bone-colored sky. The absence of the Root-Song as physical pressure.

enemies.name: Doom Herald
enemies.special: Doom Pulse: on hit, DoomClock advances by 1. Chant: +1 to all nearby enemy attack rolls. Dread Aura: DC 13 Grit or -1d6 to all rolls while in combat with a Herald.
enemies.notes: Hollowed Seekers. DR 2 (corroded Tier III armor). Carries Tier III gear but cannot use gear traits. On death: the armor is recoverable but Blight-tainted. Requires Greater Rot-Salve to cleanse for use.

loot.name: Corroded Seeker Armor
loot.description: Blight-tainted Tier III armor. Requires Greater Rot-Salve to cleanse before use. Once cleansed: Chest slot. DR 2. +1 Defense. Carries the memory of someone who did not come back.

hazards.name: Ash Inhalation
hazards.description: The Charred Wastes have no cover, no shade, no shelter. Characters without head slot armor take 1 damage per room from ash inhalation. Continuous environmental hazard.

event_triggers: On entry: The Charred Wastes have no cover, no shade, no shelter. The ash underfoot is warm from residual Blight energy. Characters without head slot armor take 1 damage per room from ash inhalation.

On Doom Heralds defeated: Their armor crashes into the ash. Voss's survival ledger recorded these names -- you recognize one. A Seeker who left Emberhome six months ago. The party can recover Tier III armor pieces (Blight-tainted, requires Greater Rot-Salve to cleanse).

1.name: The Boneyard
1.description: A depression in the wasteland where the ash thins and the underlying root-bark is exposed, scored and cracked by decades of Blight erosion. Scattered across the depression: the remains of previous expeditions. Armor fragments, shattered weapons, skeletal remains half-consumed by grey fungal growth. Three Plague Knights stand among the dead, their bodies more Blight than flesh, their armor fused permanently to their frames. They stand in formation. They remember formation. They remember nothing else.
1.read_aloud: A graveyard of ambition. The ash has blown thin here, exposing the root-bark beneath and everything that has accumulated on it: armor plate, shattered swords, a shield with Emberhome's crest half-melted by Blight acid, and bones -- bones everywhere, grey-streaked and fungal-webbed, arranged in patterns that suggest the bodies fell in formation and the Blight rearranged them afterward. Three figures stand among the dead. They are not looting. They are standing guard. Over what? Over the failure. Over the proof that no one gets past this point.
1.sensory_signature: Exposed root-bark. The clink of old armor in the wind. The silence of accumulated defeat.

enemies.name: Plague Knight
enemies.special: DR 2. Rot Strike: on hit, DC 14 Grit or take 1d4 necrotic at start of each turn for 3 rounds. Parry: +2 AC as reaction. Formation: while 2+ Knights stand, they share a damage pool -- damage is split between them. Breaking formation (killing one) causes the remainder to fight individually.
enemies.notes: Tier III-IV scavengers. Fused armor. Blight Strike: DC 14 Grit or early Blight infection.

loot.name: Rotbane Longbow
loot.description: R.Hand. Ranged. +3d6 WITS pool. [Rotbane]: attacks against Blight-type creatures ignore DR. Found among the expedition remains -- the only weapon that survived the Blight's consumption. Its string is amber-treated. It still hums.

event_triggers: On all Knights defeated: The formation breaks. The bodies collapse into the ash. Among the remains, you find expedition journals -- fragmentary, Blight-damaged, but readable. They describe the Spire's interior in partial detail: the ground floor hall, the spiral stairs, and a 'laboratory where the answers are.' The last journal entry: 'The Rot Hunter is real. We heard it above us. It does not come down. It waits for you to come up.'

On investigation DC 15: One set of remains wears a pendant -- Emberhome's Seeker crest, but older, from before Voss's tenure. The name etched into the back: 'Kaelen.' Elder Kaelen's son. He never told anyone his son came here.

2.name: The Rot River
2.description: A channel of liquid corruption flowing from the Spire's base, cutting across the approach like a moat. The liquid is not water -- it is concentrated Blight in fluid form, grey-green and viscous, giving off fumes that blur vision and burn exposed skin. The surface roils periodically as Rot Geysers erupt from subsurface pressure, throwing columns of corrosive fluid twenty feet into the air. There is no bridge. There is no ford. Crossing requires either a skill challenge or a creative solution.
2.read_aloud: A river of corruption. Grey-green, viscous, steaming. The liquid Blight flows from the Spire's base in a channel carved into dead root-bark, and the fumes rising from its surface make the air shimmer and your eyes water. As you watch, the surface bulges -- a geyser erupts, throwing a column of corrosive fluid high enough to hit the ash-clouds above. It falls back in a rain of grey droplets that sizzle where they land. The far bank is thirty feet away. The Spire looms behind it.
2.sensory_signature: Viscous grey-green flow. Burning fumes. The periodic eruption of geysers.

hazards.name: Rot Geyser
hazards.description: Erupts every 2 rounds. All characters within 20 feet of the riverbank must DC 16 Grit or take 3d8 necrotic damage. Characters in the river take 3d8 automatically per round plus Blight infection (no save).
hazards.condition: DC 14 Grit or Blight infection (immediate stage 1)

hazards.name: Rot River Crossing
hazards.description: Skill Challenge: 3 successes before 3 failures. Options: (1) DC 14 Might grappling hook swing. (2) DC 16 Wits time the geyser pattern. (3) DC 14 Aether calm the river with Root-Song. Failure: fall in, 3d8 damage, DC 12 Might rescue. Alternative: Emberheart Lantern parts the river for 1 round.

event_triggers: Hazard: Rot Geyser. Erupts every 2 rounds. All characters within 20 feet of the riverbank must DC 16 Grit or take 3d8 necrotic damage. Characters in the river take 3d8 automatically per round plus Blight infection (no save).

Skill Challenge: Crossing the Rot River. Three successes needed before three failures. Options: (1) DC 14 Might to throw a grappling hook. (2) DC 16 Wits to time the geyser pattern. (3) DC 14 Aether to calm the river temporarily.
Alternative: If carrying Emberheart Lantern from Rotwood Hollow, its fire-light parts the river for 1 round. The lantern dims permanently after this use.

3.name: The Spire Base
3.description: The Rot Spire rises from the Burnwillow's deepest root junction -- the place where all four Grove-roads once met, now sealed and corrupted beyond recognition. The Spire's exterior is organic: fungal flesh over a framework of consumed trees, its surface textured with bioluminescent nodules that pulse in arterial patterns. An archway at the base stands open. Not invitingly. The way a trap stands open. The interior is visible: a vast hall of grey stone and fungal pillars, lit by Blight-light that casts no shadows.
3.read_aloud: The Spire. It is not a building. It is a growth -- a fungal eruption the size of a cathedral, its surface alive with bioluminescent patterns that pulse like the heartbeat of something enormous. An archway stands open at its base. The interior glows grey-green. You can see pillars inside, carved from consumed root-material, and at the far end, a stairway spiraling upward. The air flowing out of the archway is warm and wet and smells of mushroom and ozone and something underneath both that you recognize but cannot name. It smells like the Root-Song would smell if the Root-Song had a smell. It smells like the tree, dying.
3.sensory_signature: Bioluminescent pulsing. Warm wet exhalation from the archway. The smell of the dying tree.

hazards.name: Contracting Archway
hazards.description: The archway contracts behind the party. Retreat is possible but costs 1 round and a DC 14 Grit check to squeeze through. The Spire wants you inside.

event_triggers: DoomClock +2 on entry. The Spire's proximity accelerates everything. All DoomClock threshold effects are in play from this point forward.
On entry: The archway contracts behind you. Not closing -- narrowing. Retreat is possible but costs 1 round and a DC 14 Grit check to squeeze through. The Spire wants you inside.
